MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jamie Kendall, Esq., of The Kendall Law Firm PC, Tel. 610.756.0200 ext. 101, for defendants Guerbet and Liebel-Flarsheim Company;
Kevin Benedicto, of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius, Tel. 415.442.1000, for defendants for Guerbet and Liebel-Flarsheim Company;
Scott Kanter, of Hinshaw, Marsh Still & Hinshaw, LLP, Tel. 408.861.6500 ext. 6541, for defendants Curascript, Inc., appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GUERBET, LLC, LIEBEL-FLARSHEIM COMPANY LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to punitive damages allegations and the prayer for punitive damages is denied as to paragraph 88 in her strict liability cause of action and her prayer and off calendar as to paragraphs 106 and 110 in her intentional misrepresentation fraud and concealment fraud claims. Ms. Simoes alleged that defendants knew that their product was unsafe and would cause death or serious physical injury to those who were exposed to the product yet failed to warn those who would be exposed to the product of the serious safety risks of the product. This allegation is sufficient to show despicable conduct carried on with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights and safety of others per Civil Code 3294(c)(1). The ruling on the moving defendants' demurrer moots the portion of this motion directed to paragraphs 106 and 110. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR No. 6840; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jamie Kendall, Esq., of The Kendall Law Firm PC, Tel. 610.756.0200 ext. 101, for defendants Guerbet and Liebel-Flarsheim Company;
Kevin Benedicto, of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius, Tel. 415.442.1000, for defendants for Guerbet and Liebel-Flarsheim Company;
Scott Kanter, of Hinshaw, Marsh Still & Hinshaw, LLP, Tel. 408.861.6500 ext. 6541, for defendants Curascript, Inc., appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GUERBET, LLC, LIEBEL-FLARSHEIM COMPANY LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the third through sixth causes of action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third through fifth causes of action for intentional misrepresentation fraud, concealment fraud and negligent misrepresentation and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act. The misrepresentation and concealment claims lack the required specificity as to what communications included a misstatement of fact and omitted material information. If Ms. Simoes contends that an Optimark label was relied on by her or her healthcare provider included a misrepresentation and/or omitted material information, she should identify the label and the misrepresentation therein and/or the material information omitted. If Ms. Simoes contends that she or her healthcare provider relied on some other communication such as advertising or product literature, she should likewise identify the other communication and the misrepresentation therein and/or the material information omitted. If she does not now have sufficient information to properly allege her fraud-based claims, she may seek to amend her complaint if she obtains such information through discovery or factual investigation. Ms. Simoes does not oppose dismissal of her CLRA claim. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR No. 6840; Dept. 302.
